text.title: Claude API Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules for production-grade Anthropic SDK bootstrap: env-only auth, full-date pinning, retry against 429 + 529, response-field cost tracking, multiprocessing safety, and `x-request-id` capture.

rule.statement: Load the API key from the `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` environment variable only; never embed it in source files, agent system prompts, or log lines.
rule.rationale: Source-embedded keys leak via VCS; prompt-embedded keys leak via logs and provider-side abuse monitoring.

rule.statement: Pin model ids with full-date suffixes (e.g. `claude-sonnet-4-20250514`); reject alias-only strings (`claude-sonnet-4`) in production configs.
rule.rationale: Aliases can resolve to a different model snapshot over time, silently changing behaviour and breaking cost-tracking lookups.

rule.statement: Wrap every Messages API call with a `tenacity` retry decorator that retries `RateLimitError` (429) AND `APIStatusError` with `status_code in {500, 502, 503, 529}` using `wait_exponential(min=1, max=60)` up to 5 attempts.
rule.rationale: 529 `overloaded_error` is a transient server condition, not a client error; applications that ignore it lose requests during peak hours.

rule.statement: Track cost using `response.model` (the response field, NOT the requested model) and the `usage.input_tokens / output_tokens / cache_creation_input_tokens / cache_read_input_tokens` quartet.
rule.rationale: Anthropic aliases may resolve to a different model snapshot than requested; using the request model causes silent cost misattribution.

rule.statement: In multiprocessing or fork-based workers, construct a fresh `Anthropic()` client per worker; do NOT share a module-level global across forks.
rule.rationale: The SDK holds httpx connection state; forked workers cannot safely share it. Sharing causes hangs and connection-reuse races.

rule.statement: Capture `x-request-id` from the response headers on every call and emit it on every error log line.
rule.rationale: Anthropic support requires `x-request-id` to trace a specific request server-side; without it production failures cannot be debugged.
